sidebar_position: 4
---

# Cache Driver Interface

Every custom cache driver must implement this interface. All built-in drivers follow this contract.

## options

Driver configuration object.

```ts
options: Options;
```

## name

Unique driver identifier.

```ts
name: string;
```

Example:
```ts
const driver = cache.driver("redis");
console.log(driver.name); // "redis"
```

## set(key, value, ttl?)

Store a value in cache. Returns the stored value.

```ts
set(key: string | GenericObject, value: any, ttl?: number): Promise<any>;
```

Example:
```ts
await cache.set("product:123", { id: 123, name: "Widget" }, 3600);
```

## get(key)

Retrieve a value from cache. Returns the value if found and not expired, otherwise `null`.

```ts
get(key: string | GenericObject): Promise<any | null>;
```

Example:
```ts
const product = await cache.get("product:123");
if (product === null) {
  console.log("Product not in cache");
}
```

## has(key)

Check if a key exists in cache without retrieving its value. Returns `true` if key exists and is not expired, `false` otherwise.

```ts
has(key: string | GenericObject): Promise<boolean>;
```

Example:
```ts
const exists = await cache.has("rate-limit:user:456");
```

## remove(key)

Delete a single key from cache. Resolves with no value.

```ts
remove(key: string | GenericObject): Promise<void>;
```

Example:
```ts
await cache.remove("product:123");
```

## flush()

Clear all cached data. Resolves with no value.

```ts
flush(): Promise<void>;
```

Example:
```ts
await cache.flush();
```

## removeNamespace(namespace)

Delete all keys within a namespace. Namespace is the first segment of a key before the first dot. Resolves with no value.

```ts
removeNamespace(namespace: string): Promise<any>;
```

Example:
```ts
// Removes all keys like "product.123", "product.456", etc.
await cache.removeNamespace("product");
```

## remember(key, ttl, callback)

Get a value from cache. If not found or expired, execute the callback function and cache the result. Includes automatic stampede prevention. Returns the cached or newly computed value.

```ts
remember(
  key: string | GenericObject,
  ttl: number,
  callback: () => Promise<any>
): Promise<any>;
```

Example:
```ts
const user = await cache.remember("user:789", 3600, async () => {
  return await database.users.find(789);
});
```

## pull(key)

Retrieve and delete a key in one atomic operation. Returns the value if found and not expired, otherwise `null`.

```ts
pull(key: string | GenericObject): Promise<any | null>;
```

Example:
```ts
const order = await cache.pull("order-session:abc123");
```

## forever(key, value)

Store a value permanently with no expiration. Returns the stored value.

```ts
forever(key: string | GenericObject, value: any): Promise<any>;
```

Example:
```ts
await cache.forever("config:app", { version: "1.0.0" });
```

## increment(key, value?)

Atomically increase a numeric value by the given amount (default 1). Returns the new value. Throws if the stored value is not numeric.

```ts
increment(key: string | GenericObject, value?: number): Promise<number>;
```

Example:
```ts
const count = await cache.increment("product:views:123", 1);
```

## decrement(key, value?)

Atomically decrease a numeric value by the given amount (default 1). Returns the new value. Throws if the stored value is not numeric.

```ts
decrement(key: string | GenericObject, value?: number): Promise<number>;
```

Example:
```ts
const stock = await cache.decrement("inventory:widget", 5);
```

## many(keys)

Retrieve multiple values in one operation. Returns an array in the same order as the input keys. Missing or expired keys return `null` in their positions.

```ts
many(keys: (string | GenericObject)[]): Promise<any[]>;
```

Example:
```ts
const [user, product, order] = await cache.many([
  "user:123",
  "product:456",
  "order:789"
]);
```

## setMany(items, ttl?)

Store multiple key-value pairs in one operation. Resolves with no value.

```ts
setMany(items: Record<string, any>, ttl?: number): Promise<void>;
```

Example:
```ts
await cache.setMany({
  "user:123": { id: 123, name: "Alice" },
  "user:456": { id: 456, name: "Bob" }
}, 3600);
```

## tags(tags)

Create a tagged cache instance to group related keys. Useful for clearing cache by category. Returns a `TaggedCacheDriver` instance.

```ts
tags(tags: string[]): TaggedCacheDriver;
```

Example:
```ts
const tagged = cache.tags(["products", "featured"]);
await tagged.set("item:1", product1);
await tagged.flush(); // Clears all items tagged with "products" and "featured"
```

## setNX(key, value, ttl?)

Atomically set a key only if it does not already exist. Returns `true` if set successfully, `false` if key already existed. Optional; Redis driver only.

```ts
setNX?(key: string | GenericObject, value: any, ttl?: number): Promise<boolean>;
```

Example:
```ts
const locked = await cache.setNX("lock:order:123", true, 30);
if (locked) {
  // Lock acquired, process order
}
```

## parseKey(key)

Normalize a cache key for driver storage. Sanitizes special characters and applies global prefix. Returns the parsed key string.

```ts
parseKey(key: string | GenericObject): string;
```

Key parsing rules:
- String keys: remove `{}"`, replace `:` and `,` with `.`
- Object keys: JSON stringify then apply string rules
- Global prefix is prepended if configured

Example:
```ts
const parsed = cache.parseKey({ namespace: "product", id: 123 });
// Might return: "app:product.123"
```

## setOptions(options)

Update driver configuration at runtime.

```ts
setOptions(options: Options): any;
```

## connect()

Establish connection to the cache backend. Resolves with any backend-specific response.

```ts
connect(): Promise<any>;
```

## disconnect()

Close connection to the cache backend. Resolves with no value.

```ts
disconnect(): Promise<void>;
```

## client

Optional reference to the underlying backend client (Redis connection, Memcached instance, etc.).

```ts
client?: ClientType;
```

## on(event, handler)

Register an event listener. Returns the driver instance for chaining.

```ts
on(event: CacheEventType, handler: CacheEventHandler): this;
```

## off(event, handler)

Unregister an event listener. Returns the driver instance for chaining.

```ts
off(event: CacheEventType, handler: CacheEventHandler): this;
```

## once(event, handler)

Register a one-time event listener that fires once then unregisters. Returns the driver instance for chaining.

```ts
once(event: CacheEventType, handler: CacheEventHandler): this;
```

## Complete Interface Definition

```ts
export interface CacheDriver<ClientType, Options> {
  options: Options;
  name: string;
  removeNamespace(namespace: string): Promise<any>;
  setOptions(options: Options): any;
  parseKey(key: string | GenericObject): string;
  set(key: string | GenericObject, value: any, ttl?: number): Promise<any>;
  get(key: string | GenericObject): Promise<any | null>;
  remove(key: string | GenericObject): Promise<void>;
  flush(): Promise<void>;
  connect(): Promise<any>;
  client?: ClientType;
  disconnect(): Promise<void>;
  has(key: string | GenericObject): Promise<boolean>;
  remember(
    key: string | GenericObject,
    ttl: number,
    callback: () => Promise<any>
  ): Promise<any>;
  pull(key: string | GenericObject): Promise<any | null>;
  forever(key: string | GenericObject, value: any): Promise<any>;
  increment(key: string | GenericObject, value?: number): Promise<number>;
  decrement(key: string | GenericObject, value?: number): Promise<number>;
  many(keys: (string | GenericObject)[]): Promise<any[]>;
  setMany(items: Record<string, any>, ttl?: number): Promise<void>;
  tags(tags: string[]): TaggedCacheDriver;
  setNX?(
    key: string | GenericObject,
    value: any,
    ttl?: number
  ): Promise<boolean>;
  on(event: CacheEventType, handler: CacheEventHandler): this;
  off(event: CacheEventType, handler: CacheEventHandler): this;
  once(event: CacheEventType, handler: CacheEventHandler): this;
}
```

## Optional Methods

`setNX()` is optional and only available on the Redis driver. Other drivers throw an error if called.

## Event Types

The event system supports the following event types:

- `hit` - Cache hit (value retrieved)
- `miss` - Cache miss (value not found or expired)
- `set` - Value stored in cache
- `removed` - Key removed from cache
- `flushed` - Entire cache cleared
- `expired` - Key expired (TTL reached)
- `connected` - Driver connected
- `disconnected` - Driver disconnected
- `error` - Error occurred during operation

See [Event System](./events) for comprehensive event documentation.

## Implementing a Custom Driver

- Extend [BaseCacheDriver](./base-cache-driver) to reuse key parsing, logging, and TTL handling
- Implement these required abstract methods: `set()`, `get()`, `remove()`, `flush()`, `removeNamespace()`
- Override `log()` for custom logging behavior
- BaseCacheDriver provides implementations for: `has()`, `remember()`, `pull()`, `forever()`, `increment()`, `decrement()`, `many()`, `setMany()`, `tags()`, and event methods

## Example Implementation

```ts
import { BaseCacheDriver } from "@warlock.js/cache";
import type { CacheDriver } from "@warlock.js/cache";

interface MyCacheOptions {
  globalPrefix?: string;
  ttl?: number;
}

export class MyCacheDriver extends BaseCacheDriver<any, MyCacheOptions> 
  implements CacheDriver<any, MyCacheOptions> {
  
  public name = "myCache";
  
  public async removeNamespace(namespace: string) {
    // Implementation for removing namespace
  }
  
  public async set(key: string | GenericObject, value: any, ttl?: number) {
    // Implementation for setting value
  }
  
  public async get(key: string | GenericObject) {
    // Implementation for getting value
  }
  
  public async remove(key: string | GenericObject) {
    // Implementation for removing value
  }
  
  public async flush() {
    // Implementation for flushing cache
  }
}
```

See [Make Your Own Cache Driver](./make-your-own-cache-driver) for a complete example.
